commit.message:
Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is
injected as OLLAMA_NUM_CTX and LM_STUDIO_NUM_CTX so both Ollama and
LM Studio use the configured context window. Values below 32768 are
clamped. The setting is saved and restored across sessions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

commit.message:
v1.22.69: Canonical address validation + sentinel zeroing on cave-fault

Three fixes for New Game crash/freeze:

1. CAVE-FAULT handler now zeros sentinel page after each redirect.
   Engine writes garbage to sentinel (RW page) that persists as
   corrupted "next" pointers (e.g. 0x574E4D454D414C43 = ASCII text),
   causing infinite loops when code follows link chains through sentinel.
   Zeroing sentinel on each fault ensures reads see NULL terminators.

2. PatchL/N/O upgraded from simple high-dword check (test r10d; jz) to
   canonical range check (dec r10d; cmp r10d, 0x7F; ja). Old check only
   rejected high_dword==0, missing garbage like 0x574E4D45 (nonzero but
   non-canonical). New check rejects both zero AND >128, catching ASCII
   text pointers that caused CAVE-FAULT cascades.

3. PatchL's invalid path now zeros RAX before jumping to function
   epilogue, signaling "not found" to the caller.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(db): create database schema with RLS policies and triggers (#30)

* feat(db): create database schema with RLS policies and triggers

- Add 9 tables: profiles, ai_tools, tech_stack_tags, builds,
  build_screenshots, build_ai_tools, build_tech_stack_tags,
  comments, upvotes
- Add build_type enum (app, feature, fix, automation, experiment)
- Enable RLS on all tables with public SELECT, owner write policies,
  and admin-only policies for ai_tools and tech_stack_tags
- Add is_admin() helper function using app_metadata JWT claim
- Add triggers: auto-create profile on signup, auto-update updated_at
- Add build-screenshots storage bucket with RLS policies
- Add seed data: 13 AI tools, 15 tech stack tags
- Generate TypeScript types and wire Database generic to Supabase clients
- Add CONTRIBUTING.md with frontend and backend onboarding guides
- Update .env.local.example with Option A (cloud) / Option B (local) setup
- Add gen-types:prod script for cloud type generation

Closes #4

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(db): address PR review issues in schema and storage migrations

- Add DEFAULT auth.uid() to builds.user_id and comments.user_id so INSERT
  RLS policies (WITH CHECK auth.uid() = user_id) pass when caller omits user_id
- Add clarifying comments documenting intentional ON DELETE SET NULL +
  DEFAULT auth.uid() coexistence on both tables
- Scope storage INSERT policy to user-owned paths via storage.foldername():
  (storage.foldername(name))[1] = (select auth.uid()::text)
- Add SECURITY INVOKER + SET search_path = '' to update_updated_at() to
  follow least-privilege and prevent CVE-2018-1058 search-path hijacking;
  qualify now() as pg_catalog.now()
- Wrap all auth.uid() and is_admin() calls in RLS policies with (select ...)
  subqueries for per-statement initPlan caching (Supabase best practice)
- Remove '' fallback from COALESCE in handle_new_user() so absent metadata
  correctly inserts NULL instead of empty string
- Add UPDATE policy for build_screenshots with USING + WITH CHECK to allow
  atomic display_order updates without delete-then-reinsert
- Add comment above builds UPDATE/DELETE policies documenting that orphaned
  builds (user_id = NULL after user deletion) require admin intervention

Closes #4

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: enterprise GUI redesign — modular pipeline stepper with 5 stages (#25)

- Split monolithic 967-line App.tsx into 11 focused modules
- Pipeline stepper: Build → Inject → Verify → Diff → Completion
- Centralized app state via useReducer (store.ts) with full job lifecycle
- Real-time structured progress: determinate bars, indeterminate animation,
  byte transfer display, elapsed timer (JobProgress.tsx)
- Distro capability model (distro.ts): Ubuntu/Mint/Fedora/Arch with
  per-operation support levels rendered in capability matrix

- Collapsible accordion sections for all 14 inject config groups
- DoctorSidebar: system health, tool availability, warnings
- LogPanel: filterable engine log bar with level filter and clear
- CompletionStage: pipeline summary with all artifacts and results
- engine/src/events.rs: substage, percent, bytes_done, bytes_total fields
- gui/src-tauri/src/main.rs: extended event payload for new fields
- Full CSS design system rewrite with design tokens (styles.css)

commit.message:
refactor: rewrite script 29 with 3-step architecture for IBKR↔Holly mapping

Step 1 - Execution Normalization:
  - Parse IBKR Flex CSV (HEADER/DATA multi-section + flat)
  - Handle EOS/EOA/EOF footer rows, CONF/TRNT section types
  - Filter to STK + EXECUTION level only
  - Cluster partial fills by OrderID → VWAP entry/exit per order
  - Pair opening/closing orders into round-trip positions (FIFO)

Step 2 - Match Engine:
  - Score candidates by (symbol, direction, time proximity, price proximity)
  - Confidence scoring: 1.0=exact → 0.0=no match
  - Auto-accept above 0.70, flag low-confidence for review queue
  - Honest language: "best-matched" not "triggered"

Step 3 - Comparison Layer:
  - Entry/exit slippage ($, %, R)
  - P&L comparison (IBKR actual vs Holly theoretical)
  - Coverage stats (matched/missed/extra)
  - By-strategy breakdown with confidence scores
  - JSON + CSV export to output/reports/trade_mapping/

Based on analysis of actual IBKR exports:
  - TradeConfirmations_FULL.csv: 4,558 STK fills, 600 symbols, Sep 2024 - Mar 2026
  - KNT example: 4 entry fills across 4 exchanges for one OrderID

セキュリティ制限でブロックされるため、クリック可能な
リンクボタンに変更

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add comprehensive .env setup guide for releases

- Add RELEASE-SETUP.md specifically for users downloading from GitHub Releases
- Clarify that .env file is intentionally excluded from releases for security
- Explain .env.template usage and manual setup process
- Update README.md to highlight RELEASE-SETUP.md guide
- Add .env file explanation section to POST-CLONE-GUIDE.md
- Improve documentation flow for both clone and release download scenarios

This addresses user confusion about missing .env files in releases by clearly documenting:
1. Why .env is excluded (security - contains sensitive API keys)
2. How to create .env from .env.template
3. Both automated (setup script) and manual setup methods

commit.message:
feat: Add Test Recording - record browser interactions and generate tests

Implement feature 2.2 from roadmap: Test Recording functionality that allows
users to record browser interactions and automatically generate play-ai or
Playwright tests.

Key features:
- CLI command: npx play-ai record <url>
- Programmatic API: startRecording(), stopRecording(), pauseRecording()
- Multiple output formats: play-ai, playwright, or both
- Smart selector generation following Playwright's recommended priority
- Browser script injection for capturing clicks, inputs, keyboard events
- Session management with undo, clear, and custom action support

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Pool QueueWithRetry and cache filterDirtyFiles regexp (#19544)

## Summary

- **QueueWithRetry pooling** (`execution/exec/txtask.go`): Add
`sync.Pool` for `QueueWithRetry` with a `Release()` method that drains
the 100K-element channel without closing it, preserving the ~1.6MB
buffer across reuses. `parallelExecutor.run` calls `Release()` instead
of `Close()`; workers exit via `ctx.Done()`. Cleanup ordering ensures
`stopWorkers()` completes before `Release()` to avoid races.
- **filterDirtyFiles regexp cache** (`db/state/dirty_files.go`): Cache
compiled regexps in a `sync.Map` keyed by pattern string. Each unique
`(filenameBase, ext)` pair compiles once instead of on every call.

Profiling testlab test runs showed QueueWithRetry allocation at 79.5GB
(18.8% of total allocs) and filterDirtyFiles regexp at 14.6GB (3.3%).
Combined expected savings: ~94GB (22% of total allocations).

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify `go build ./...` passes
- [ ] Run `go test ./execution/exec/...` and `go test
./execution/stagedsync/...` and `go test ./db/state/...`
- [ ] Run full CI suite to check for regressions

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Alexey Sharp <alexeysharp@Mac-mini.local>
